title: Create base analyzer interface
description:

Acceptance Criteria:
- BaseAnalyzer ABC defined
- AnalysisResult Pydantic model defined

closeComment:
✅ Implemented SHAP and LIME explainability modules.

**SHAP module** (`src/explainability/shap_explainer.py`):
- TreeSHAP for RandomForest, KernelSHAP fallback for XGBoost, LinearSHAP for LR
- `explain_prediction()` → structured dict with top positive/negative contributions, risk level
- `get_global_importance()` → DataFrame of mean |SHAP| feature importance
- Plot functions: waterfall, summary (beeswarm), bar importance
- Handles SHAP 0.49 3D output format

**LIME module** (`src/explainability/lime_explainer.py`):
- `LimeExplainerWrapper` class for model-agnostic local explanations
- Consistent API with SHAP module for easy comparison

Commit: 940570e

title: Implement NLP analyzer with spaCy

- Returns AnalysisResult with reasons

closeComment:
✅ Implemented prediction module and Flask backend.

**Prediction module** (`src/prediction/`):
- `predictor.py` — `CreditRiskPredictor` class: validate → transform → predict → explain
- `input_validator.py` — validates 20 features (range checks, category validation)
- Singleton pattern for efficient web serving (model loaded once)

**Flask endpoints** (`web/app.py`):
- `POST /predict` — full prediction with SHAP explanation
- `GET /schema` — dynamic form schema for frontend
- `GET /models` — list available trained models
- `GET /health` — health check

title: Initialize Next.js 15 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui project

- shadcn/ui initialized with `npx shadcn@latest init`

- Folder structure: app/, components/, lib/, hooks/, types/
- ESLint + Prettier configured
- Base components installed: Button, Card, Input, Badge, Tabs
- Runs on `http://localhost:3000`
- Old placeholder frontend/ files removed or archived

title: Implement responsive design for mobile and tablet
description:
Ensure the entire application works beautifully on all screen sizes.

Breakpoints:
- Mobile: < 640px (single column, hamburger menu, stacked cards)
- Tablet: 640px - 1024px (2-column grid, collapsible sidebar)
- Desktop: > 1024px (full sidebar, 3-column grids, spacious layout)

Mobile-specific:
- Hamburger menu replaces sidebar
- Swipe gestures for tabs
- Touch-friendly button sizes (min 44px)
- Bottom navigation bar option
- Full-width cards
- Simplified charts

Acceptance Criteria:
- Every page tested at 375px, 768px, 1024px, 1440px widths
- No horizontal scrolling at any breakpoint
- Touch targets ≥ 44x44px
- Text remains readable without zooming
- Charts resize properly
- Forms are usable on mobile keyboards
- Tailwind responsive utilities used consistently

title: Add accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA) compliance
description:
Ensure the application meets WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility standards — important for thesis quality.

Requirements:
- Keyboard navigation: All interactive elements focusable and operable
- Screen reader support: Proper ARIA labels, roles, and live regions
- Color contrast: All text meets 4.5:1 ratio (AA)
- Focus indicators: Visible focus rings on all interactive elements
- Alt text: All images and icons have descriptive alt text
- Semantic HTML: Proper heading hierarchy, landmarks, lists
- Form labels: All inputs have associated labels
- Error announcements: Form errors announced to screen readers
- Skip navigation link
- Reduced motion support

Acceptance Criteria:
- `components/ui/skipNav.tsx` — Skip to main content link
- Lighthouse accessibility score ≥ 90
- All shadcn components already have built-in a11y (verify)
- Custom components tested with screen reader
- Color-blind safe color palette (not relying on color alone)
- Keyboard shortcut help dialog (?)

title: Configure FastAPI CORS for frontend integration
description:
Update the FastAPI backend to allow requests from the Next.js frontend.

Changes:
- Update `backend/main.py` CORS middleware:
  - Allow origin: `http://localhost:3000` (dev)
  - Allow methods: GET, POST, OPTIONS
  - Allow headers: Content-Type, Authorization
  - Allow credentials: true
- Add CORS_ORIGINS to config.py Settings (env variable)
- Add health check response headers

Acceptance Criteria:
- Frontend can call all 5 API endpoints without CORS errors
- CORS origins configurable via environment variable
- Preflight OPTIONS requests handled correctly
- No wildcard (*) in production
- Tested with actual frontend-to-backend calls

title: Add real-time analysis progress feedback via SSE or polling
description:
Provide real-time progress updates to the user while analysis is running.

Approach (choose one):
- **Option A: Server-Sent Events (SSE)** — Backend streams progress events
- **Option B: Polling** — Frontend polls a status endpoint every 500ms

Progress Steps to Show:
1. 'Extracting URL features...' (0-20%)
2. 'Running OSINT checks...' (20-60%)
3. 'Analyzing text with NLP...' (60-80%)
4. 'Calculating final score...' (80-95%)
5. 'Analysis complete!' (100%)

Acceptance Criteria:
- `components/analyze/progressBar.tsx` — Animated step progress
- Progress bar with labeled steps
- Estimated time remaining
- Graceful fallback if progress tracking unavailable
- Works with existing backend (minimal backend changes)
- Cancel analysis button

title: Build Settings page with API configuration
description:
Create a settings page for configuring the application.

Settings:
1. **API Configuration**
   - Backend API URL input (default: http://localhost:8000)
   - Test Connection button (calls /api/health)
   - Connection status indicator (green/red dot)

2. **Display Preferences**
   - Theme (Light / Dark / System)
   - Language (English — extensible)
   - Results detail level (Simple / Detailed / Expert)

3. **History Settings**
   - Max history entries (10/25/50/100)
   - Auto-clear after N days
   - Clear all history button

4. **About Section**
   - App version, author, supervisor
   - ELTE branding
   - Link to thesis

Acceptance Criteria:
- `app/settings/page.tsx`
- Settings persisted in localStorage
- `lib/storage/settingsStore.ts` — Settings CRUD
- shadcn Form, Select, Switch, Input components
- Changes apply immediately (no save button needed)
- Reset to defaults button

title: Write frontend unit tests with Jest + React Testing Library
description:
Comprehensive unit tests for all frontend components.

Test Files:
- `__tests__/components/sidebar.test.tsx`
- `__tests__/components/verdictBanner.test.tsx`
- `__tests__/components/osintCards.test.tsx`
- `__tests__/components/featureCards.test.tsx`
- `__tests__/components/historyTable.test.tsx`
- `__tests__/components/scoreBreakdown.test.tsx`
- `__tests__/lib/apiClient.test.ts`
- `__tests__/lib/historyStore.test.ts`
- `__tests__/lib/settingsStore.test.ts`

Coverage Targets:
- Components: 80%+ coverage
- API client: 90%+ coverage
- Storage utilities: 90%+ coverage

Acceptance Criteria:
- Jest + React Testing Library configured
- Mock API responses for component tests
- Snapshot tests for key components
- User interaction tests (click, type, submit)
- Accessibility tests (screen reader assertions)
- All tests pass in CI
- `npm test` runs all tests

title: Write end-to-end tests with Playwright
description:
E2E tests that verify the full user journey from input to results.

Test Scenarios:
1. **URL Analysis Flow**: Enter URL → Submit → See results → Check verdict
2. **Email Analysis Flow**: Enter email → Submit → See results
3. **Navigation**: Click all nav links → Verify pages load
4. **History**: Analyze URL → Go to history → See entry → Click view
5. **Theme Toggle**: Switch dark/light → Verify persistence on reload
6. **Settings**: Change API URL → Test connection → Verify
7. **Error Handling**: Submit with backend down → See error toast
8. **Responsive**: Run scenarios at mobile viewport
9. **Keyboard Navigation**: Tab through entire app
10. **Empty States**: Visit history with no data → See illustration

Acceptance Criteria:
- Playwright installed and configured
- `e2e/` directory with all test files
- Tests run against local dev server
- Mock backend for deterministic results
- Screenshots on failure
- `npm run test:e2e` command
- All 10 scenarios pass

title: Add batch URL analysis feature
description:
Allow users to analyze multiple URLs at once — a power feature for the thesis demo.

Features:
- **Bulk Input**: Textarea where users can paste multiple URLs (one per line)
- **File Upload**: Upload a .txt or .csv file of URLs
- **Batch Progress**: Show individual progress for each URL
- **Results Table**: All results in a sortable table
- **Summary Stats**: X safe, Y suspicious, Z dangerous, W critical
- **Export**: Download all results as CSV or JSON

Backend:
- Use existing `/api/analyze/url` endpoint in a loop
- Client-side concurrency: max 3 parallel requests
- Rate limiting awareness

Acceptance Criteria:
- `app/analyze/batch/page.tsx`
- `components/analyze/batchInput.tsx`
- `components/analyze/batchResults.tsx`
- Max 50 URLs per batch
- Progress tracking per URL
- Error handling per URL (one failure doesn't stop batch)
- Export to CSV with all fields
- Cancel batch button

title: Create custom phishing shield logo and branding assets
description:
Design a professional logo and branding for the thesis application.

Assets Needed:
- App logo (shield + magnifying glass concept) as SVG
- Favicon (16x16, 32x32, 192x192, 512x512)
- Open Graph image (1200x630) for link previews
- Loading animation (animated shield)
- Empty state illustrations (no history, no results)
- Threat level icons (safe shield, warning triangle, danger skull, critical fire)

Branding:
- App name: 'PhishGuard' or 'OSINT Shield' (thesis branding)
- Color palette derived from shadcn theme
- ELTE university colors as accent

Acceptance Criteria:
- `public/` directory with all assets
- SVG logo renders at all sizes
- Favicon configured in Next.js metadata
- OG image configured for social sharing
- All icons consistent in style (Lucide-compatible)
- Professional, thesis-worthy appearance

title: Implement toast notification system
description:
Add a toast notification system for user feedback throughout the app.

Toast Types:
- ✅ Success (green): 'Analysis complete!', 'Settings saved'
- ❌ Error (red): 'API connection failed', 'Analysis error'
- ⚠️ Warning (yellow): 'Backend is degraded', 'Rate limit approaching'
- ℹ️ Info (blue): 'Tip: You can analyze emails too!', 'New feature available'

Behavior:
- Auto-dismiss after 5 seconds
- Manual dismiss with X button
- Stack multiple toasts
- Position: bottom-right
- Animate in/out

Acceptance Criteria:
- shadcn Sonner (toast) component installed
- `lib/toast.ts` — Helper functions: showSuccess(), showError(), showWarning(), showInfo()
- Used throughout the app for all user feedback
- Non-blocking (doesn't cover important UI)
- Accessible (announced to screen readers)

title: Add copy-to-clipboard and share functionality for results
description:
Allow users to copy and share analysis results.

Features:
- **Copy Results** button — Copies formatted text summary to clipboard
- **Copy JSON** button — Copies raw API response as JSON
- **Share Link** — Generate a shareable link (URL with encoded params)
- **Print** button — Print-optimized CSS for results page
- **Download PDF** — Export results as a formatted PDF report

Acceptance Criteria:
- `components/results/shareActions.tsx`
- Clipboard API with fallback for older browsers
- Toast notification on successful copy
- Print CSS media query for clean printout
- PDF generation using browser print or html2canvas+jsPDF
- Share button with copy-link functionality
- All buttons have proper icons and tooltips

title: Add keyboard shortcuts throughout the application
description:
Power-user keyboard shortcuts for fast navigation and actions.

Shortcuts:
- `Ctrl+K` or `/` — Focus search/URL input
- `Ctrl+Enter` — Submit analysis form
- `Ctrl+Shift+D` — Toggle dark mode
- `Ctrl+H` — Go to history
- `Ctrl+N` — New analysis
- `?` — Show keyboard shortcuts help dialog
- `Escape` — Close modals/dialogs

Acceptance Criteria:
- `hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts` — Custom hook
- `components/ui/shortcutsDialog.tsx` — Help dialog listing all shortcuts
- Shortcuts don't conflict with browser defaults
- Shortcuts disabled when typing in input fields
- Shortcuts shown as hints on buttons (e.g., 'Analyze (Ctrl+Enter)')
- Accessible (not relying on shortcuts for core functionality)

title: Optimize frontend performance and bundle size
description:
Ensure the frontend is fast and optimized for production.

Optimizations:
- Next.js Image component for all images
- Dynamic imports for heavy components (charts, tables)
- Route-based code splitting (automatic with App Router)
- Font optimization (next/font)
- Metadata optimization for SEO
- Bundle analysis with @next/bundle-analyzer
- Lighthouse performance score ≥ 90

Performance Targets:
- First Contentful Paint < 1.5s
- Largest Contentful Paint < 2.5s
- Time to Interactive < 3s
- Bundle size < 200KB (first load JS)

Acceptance Criteria:
- `next.config.ts` optimized
- Dynamic imports for Recharts, TanStack Table
- Font preloading configured
- No unnecessary re-renders (React DevTools verified)
- Lighthouse audit ≥ 90 performance
- Bundle analysis report generated

title: Add backend analysis history endpoint with in-memory store
description:
Add a lightweight backend endpoint to store and retrieve analysis history.

Endpoints:
- `GET /api/history` — Return list of past analyses (last 100)
- `GET /api/history/{id}` — Return single analysis by ID
- `DELETE /api/history/{id}` — Delete single entry
- `DELETE /api/history` — Clear all history

Storage:
- In-memory store (list) — no database needed for thesis
- Each analysis auto-saved after completion
- UUID for each analysis ID
- Max 100 entries with FIFO eviction

Acceptance Criteria:
- `backend/api/historyStore.py` — In-memory CRUD
- New routes in router.py
- Pydantic schemas for history responses
- Unit tests for store operations
- Integration tests for endpoints
- Analysis ID returned in AnalysisResponse (add field)

title: Write Milestone 3 methodology and results draft
description:
Draft the methodology and preliminary results sections of the thesis.

Sections to Draft:
1. **System Architecture** — Diagram + description of 3-layer pipeline
2. **Implementation Methodology**
   - Technology choices and justification (Next.js, FastAPI, spaCy, etc.)
   - Design patterns used (DI, Protocol-based, MVC)
   - Development workflow (Git, issues, MCP servers)
3. **NLP Feature Extraction** — spaCy pipeline details
4. **OSINT Integration** — Data sources and enrichment process
5. **Scoring Algorithm** — Mathematical formulation with weights
6. **Frontend Architecture** — Component hierarchy, state management
7. **Preliminary Results** — Test against known phishing/safe URLs:
   - Accuracy metrics
   - False positive/negative analysis
   - Performance benchmarks (response times)
8. **Evaluation Plan** — How the system will be evaluated in M4

Acceptance Criteria:
- `docs/milestones/milestone-3.md` — Updated with all work done
- `docs/methodology-draft.md` — 3-5 page draft
- Architecture diagrams updated
- Results tables with real test data
- Ready for supervisor review

title: Create developer setup documentation for frontend
description:
Document how to set up and run the complete project (frontend + backend).

Documentation:
1. **Prerequisites** — Node.js, Python, npm/pnpm versions
2. **Backend Setup** — venv, pip install, run server
3. **Frontend Setup** — npm install, environment variables, run dev
4. **Running Both** — Concurrent dev servers
5. **Environment Variables** — All env vars documented
6. **Testing** — How to run frontend + backend tests
7. **Building for Production** — next build, deployment notes
8. **Troubleshooting** — Common issues and solutions

Acceptance Criteria:
- README.md updated with full setup instructions
- `frontend/README.md` — Frontend-specific docs
- `.env.example` files for both frontend and backend
- New developer can set up the entire project in < 10 minutes
- All commands tested on fresh clone

title: Final integration testing — frontend ↔ backend end-to-end
description:
Comprehensive integration testing of the complete system.

Test Matrix:
1. **URL Analysis E2E**: Frontend → POST /api/analyze/url → Display results
2. **Email Analysis E2E**: Frontend → POST /api/analyze/email → Display results
3. **Auto-detect E2E**: Frontend → POST /api/analyze (auto) → Correct detection
4. **Health Check**: Frontend dashboard → GET /api/health → Show status
5. **Error Scenarios**:
   - Backend down → Frontend shows error gracefully
   - Invalid URL → Frontend shows validation error
   - Empty content → Frontend prevents submission
   - Timeout → Frontend shows timeout message
6. **History Flow**: Analyze → Save → View history → Click → See results
7. **Batch Analysis**: Multiple URLs → All analyzed → Results table
8. **Cross-browser**: Chrome, Firefox, Safari (if available)

Acceptance Criteria:
- All 8 test scenarios pass
- Both servers running simultaneously
- No console errors in browser
- No unhandled promise rejections
- Response times < 5 seconds for single analysis
- All test results documented
